claim: Poe functions as browser/interface for AI: users choose which models/agents to use, monetize bots, share agents; similar to how web browser democratized internet
actionable: Build AI products as agents/bots on Poe platform rather than standalone apps; leverage Poe's distribution, payment, and cross-model access
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt

claim: Reasoning models (o3, o4-mini, Claude 3.7, Gemini 2.5, DeepSeek) growing fastest in usage; especially powerful for code generation tasks
actionable: Prioritize testing and optimizing prompts for reasoning models; they provide superior accuracy on complex tasks, especially code generation
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt

claim: Text models still dominate usage over image/video; image models not yet reliable enough for professional graphics; text quality far exceeds human capability in value
actionable: Focus AI product investments on text-based applications; image/video models still lack fidelity for professional use despite recent improvements
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt

claim: Users can query multiple models in parallel on Poe using @model syntax; allows side-by-side comparison of outputs across different models
actionable: Use Poe's @model syntax to stack multiple model mentions in single message for parallel execution and comparison of outputs
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt

claim: Prompt bots (simple prompt + model) are created by non-technical users with strong empathy for how models work; marketing and persistence key to success
actionable: Develop prompt engineering as primary skill; non-technical people can earn hundreds of thousands annually creating popular prompt bots on Poe
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt

claim: Server bots (HTTP endpoint) enable agents and complex workflows; Poe creators earning millions annually through agent bots, trained models, or optimized inference
actionable: Build server bots for agent workflows or model hosting on Poe; reach global audience and earn recurring revenue through bot monetization
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt

claim: Current Poe bots are mostly read-only (no side effects); agents with side effects coming but blocked by infrastructure priorities; MCP helps standardize this
actionable: Current Poe bots cannot modify external systems; plan bot design assuming read-only constraints; MCP adoption pending for future action capabilities
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt

claim: Building AI products requires 2-month planning horizon vs 2-year for traditional products; environment changes too fast for longer planning
actionable: Adopt agile 2-month planning cycles for AI products; plan too far ahead means plans become irrelevant due to model/capability changes
source_title: From Quora to Poe: Adam D'Angelo on Building Platforms for LLMs and Agents | LangChain Interrupt
